title: Live /restart did not reach WatchTower supervised restart
evidence: No new HASHI9 main.py start time
No new HASHI9 remote start time
resolution: Initial report was explained by old HASHI9 runtime not loading /restart. WatchTower restart route later executed successfully after launcher fixes.

title: WatchTower service-context startup did not restore HASHI9 healthy
evidence: restart-4a0f6144c3bc4b9a9c4dfa4f7b9e6b99 showed direct bridge-u.bat lacked claude PATH and did not become healthy
restart-8a250dbe1abb4d29ba5e3bcbc2cf5141 failed_verify with PowerShell bridge_ctl in service context
resolution: WatchTower now starts HASHI9 via fixed direct python main.py launcher with service-safe PATH and BRIDGE_HOME.

title: Direct launcher initially used repo root instead of hashi9-home
resolution: WatchTower now infers C:\Users\thene\hashi9-home when present and passes it as --bridge-home.

title: WatchTower service restart launched HASHI9 without user Codex home environment
evidence: User reported codex-cli websocket 401 Unauthorized after /restart
C:\Users\thene\.codex\auth.json existed, but service-launched HASHI did not expose the expected user profile context
req-0001/req-0002 failed via codex-cli after restart
resolution: WatchTower Windows start environment now sets USERPROFILE, HOME, APPDATA, LOCALAPPDATA, and CODEX_HOME from the inferred user home. After restart, codex-cli gpt-5.4 smoke completed successfully.
